Understanding Microservices Architecture

By Frank Architect July 20, 2024

Microservices architecture has become the de facto standard for building scalable applications. But is it always the right choice?

In this article, we'll explore the trade-offs and when monoliths might actually be better.

  • Grace Ops July 20, 2024

    Finally someone who admits monoliths aren't always bad. Our team wasted months splitting a simple CRUD app into microservices.

    • Frank Architect July 20, 2024

      Exactly! Start with a modular monolith and split only when you have a clear scaling need.

  • Henry DevOps July 21, 2024

    The operational overhead of microservices is often underestimated. You need solid CI/CD, observability, and service mesh.

  • Iris Engineer July 22, 2024

    Great balanced take. Shared this with my team who are considering a migration.
